name: Di tích Lịch sử Thành cổ Quảng Trị

reviews.author: Gaby Gigi Vo
reviews.text: A historic place to pay tribute to soldiers who sacrified their loves in the 81-day battle in 1972.

reviews.author: Elaine McCafferty
reviews.text: A simple yet moving tribute to the brave soldiers who fought for this strategic citadel.

reviews.author: Jackson Le
reviews.text: Visiting Thành Cổ Quảng Trị isn’t just about sightseeing it’s about stepping onto sacred ground, where history is etched into every stone and whispering breeze. This site, once the epicenter of one of the bloodiest battles of the Vietnam War, now stands as a memorial to the thousands of soldiers who fought and fell here, on both sides. The atmosphere is somber yet peaceful, a stark contrast to the devastation it once witnessed. Walking through the site, you’ll see monuments, engraved names, and relics that tell a story of resilience, sacrifice, and the brutal reality of war. The preserved walls and surrounding landscape serve as a silent reminder of the immense loss suffered during the 81-day siege of 1972, when the entire town became a battlefield. This is a place for reflection and respect. Whether you have a personal connection to Vietnam’s history or are simply a visitor seeking to understand its past, Thành Cổ Quảng Trị leaves an impression that lingers long after you leave. A visit here isn’t just about learning history—it’s about feeling it.

reviews.author: Nguyễn Trang
reviews.text: I watched The Scent of Burning Grass and always wanted to be here one day. I was crying and felt the sacred atmosphere here. You should have tour guide with you to understand more about this place. Rest in peace and appreciate all young soldiers that sacrificed for the freedom I have today

reviews.author: Chinh Nguyen
reviews.text: The most famous and historical place in Quang Tri town (previously). The symbol of strong survival of Vietnamese people through the tough time 81 days against the enemy. Refrain to visit here during special occasion like independent day Sep 2nd or Jul 27th… there will be huge people here.

reviews.author: Công Thiện Trương
reviews.text: Historic place, here lies too many fallen soldiers of the heroic Vietnamese generation, some were still too young (15-18 year old). This is to show the world that Viet Nam is an unyielding country, and even with America’s interference, nothing cannot make us give up on our way to unite the country as a whole.

reviews.author: Thy Tran
reviews.text: It was so touchful to visit this place. I got a chance to understand more about history of my dear Viet Nam. People from the past, fought by all their lives, just to gain peace for us - our young generation. I almost cried when reading the letter from a sodier who wrote about his coming dead, left his last words to his family...If you love hisfory, please do not skip this place.

reviews.author: Abraham Arcloin
reviews.text: This is an ancient city, but now its more important significance is the resistance in the war.This castle was basically completely destroyed during the war with France. Now many schools or units use it as a patriotic education base. Hope it can be better protected and developed Tickets are free and a quick visit only takes 15 minutes.

reviews.author: Mekong Discovery
reviews.text: Quang Tri Ancient Citadel is situated in the heart of Quang Tri Town, which was once a military defense system and hosted the administration office of the Nguyen Dynasty (1802-1945). The site was taken over by the American-backed southern regime. It witnessed fierce battles during the American War in 1968 and 1972 between the American-backed forces and the southern liberation force led by Ho Chi Minh's government. After the fights between June 28 and September 16 in 1972, when thousands of soldiers of both sides died, the whole citadel was almost destroyed. There remained only the remnant of the east gate and some walls. The site has been upgraded as a historical relic site with some rebuilt wall sections, four main gates and a monument commemorating 81 days and nights of fierce fighting in 1972.

reviews.author: Adam Yan
reviews.text: Off the beaten track, this place is special thanks to its history. I was glad to witness this important historical complex in Quang Tri.

reviews.author: Dinh Hoàng
reviews.text: This is the visualized tomb for over 10000 VN soilders died during 81 days when they were protecting the fort of Quang Tri in VN war. Their corpses never be found because tons of bomb were dropped at last. Their dedication become eternity.

reviews.author: nhan hoang
reviews.text: Historic land. Respect and gratitude.

reviews.author: Huey Meeker, MD
reviews.text: Just wanted to stop by the old Quang Tri city where the fierce battle in the summer 1972 claimed thousands of lives after 82 days

reviews.author: Dederick /
reviews.text: This tiny Hidden gem of ruins from an old fort. Is great to have a peak when you are nearby! The art displayed and well kept garden are an nice extra to the history behind the fort.

reviews.author: Đức Đỗ Đăng Minh
reviews.text: Good, modern The hygiene is not good as there is some rubbish left. Besides, the awareness of some visitors are very bad as they talk loudly some information instead of keeping silent, wear short skirts which affect for polite visitor.

reviews.author: Huyen Tran
reviews.text: A must visit place when you are in Quang Tri. Historical place, free entry ticket. It is located in the bank of Thanh Han river, which was the site of firece battles during the war with the US. The site became the tomb of tens of thousands of Vietnamese soldiers who sacrificed their lives for the national liberation.

reviews.author: Ho Ngoc Tin
reviews.text: The wall has almost been destroyed by War bombs. But you should to come here and see how we fought against French and American in the past.

reviews.author: Duc Hanh NGUYEN
reviews.text: A must visit historical site, a memorable DMZ, a fierce battle field during the war against American army. This is the land of heroes' blood and lives as well as bombs.

reviews.author: Jason Sioutis
reviews.text: Amazing place with a deep history. The first official front of the war.

reviews.author: Simple Life
reviews.text: a place that too many Vietcong soldier has sacrificed themselves too protect their country. Dont forget them, they are hero!!!!

reviews.author: Johan Groen
reviews.text: Visit only if you have memories here.

reviews.author: Ján M
reviews.text: A sacred place where a lot of vietnamese soldiers and civilians fell. Do wear long trousers here. Not a lot of buffet options so bring your own water and food, especially if you re going with guided tour through whole DMZ. There is a small museum. Behave and respect! Not so much tourists mainly inland vietnamese tourists.

reviews.author: Colin T
reviews.text: Spent an hour there. Very informative place if you take the time.

reviews.author: Nguyen Van Hien
reviews.text: Not much to see. The citadel was destroyed. Everything is newly built. Won't come back

reviews.author: A Xoi
reviews.text: A Historic Place in Middle of VN

reviews.author: Thuy Nguyen
reviews.text: A sacred place to worship the dead soldiers who died for the war to get independence for them one Vietnam. There is a museum where the war photos and documentary film is showed for visitors. The pass is so depressing if to know how many people died in the war and how hard people’ lives during that period of only fire and fight

reviews.author: nick bali
reviews.text: Don't miss it- so peaceful now after thousands of tons of bombs totally anhialated this area just 50 ish short years ago

reviews.author: QuanggVN
reviews.text: a place to commemorate all the soldiers who fought during the Resistance War against America

reviews.author: Ngoc Loi
reviews.text: the ancient citadel, the fierce battle of 81 days and nights in 1972 in the war against the American imperialists to protect the country. The place of sacrifice of so many soldiers. Ancient Citadel is considered by the local people as "Spiritual Land" because there are bombs and bones of soldiers on both sides of the country. Currently the largest park in Quang Tri Town. When come here, you need to visit the museum to better understand its history.
